message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria (Indio)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~344 reviews; TripAdvisor ~79 reviews; Restaurantji ~161 reviews. Google review count [UNVERIFIED] but rating reported ~4.3.
- Sentiment: mostly positive overall, but mixed-to-declining in recent reviews
- Longevity: Founded 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella (relocated from NJ); ~60 years operating. Current operators Ron and Norma Hare continue the tradition. Ownership has transitioned from founding family but the brand has remained continuous.
- Credentials: ["Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant (self-reported / reflected on Visit Greater Palm Springs listing)","Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Pizza (self-reported / reflected on Visit Greater Palm Springs listing)","TripAdvisor ranking #22 of 165 Indio restaurants","Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listing"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best Italian food in Indio' (named first on Coachella Valley Locals best-of list) and 'Italian restaurant Coachella Valley' (named in Visit Greater Palm Springs / search summaries as the favorite Italian and favorite pizza). Does NOT surface for 'best pizza Coachella Valley' top lists (TripAdvisor/Yelp lists return Piero's, SPC, Upper Crust, Sabatinos, Your Pie instead). Does NOT surface for 'date night Coachella Valley' (Italica and Palm Springs Life lists dominate). Absent from Palm Springs Life 'That's Amore' Italian roundup in search snippets.
- Site structured data: Own domain cirosindio.com would not load via WebFetch (socket closed / 403) across multiple attempts — indicates the site is either intermittently available, blocking bots, or lightly maintained. Schema.org / JSON-LD presence [UNVERIFIED]. NAP signals are scattered across third-party aggregators (Yelp, TripAdvisor, OpenTable, Grubhub, Seamless, UberEats, SinglePlatform, Wheree, Restaurantji, Visit Greater Palm Springs, Facebook x2, Nextdoor) with inconsistent name spellings (Ciro's vs Ciros, Ristorante vs Restorante) and at least one stale 'CLOSED' flag on Yellow Pages.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's the information about Ciro's Ristorante Pizzeria in Indio:

## History and Founding

Ciro's Ristorante and Pizzeria was opened in 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella. The Mazzellas moved to the desert after many years as owners and operators of successful Italian Restaurants in New Jersey.

## Restaurant Development

Over the years other family members joined in the growth of the business. The menu was developed by request of the customers, so everything that is available is tried and accepted over the many years.

## Current Leadership and Recognition

Ron and Norma Hare continue the tradition today. The restaurant has achieved significant recognition in the local community - it has been voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant and Favorite Pizza.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Italian restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Indio area for 2026:

## Top Recommendations:

**Italica (Indio)** - An intimate, speakeasy-style restaurant with moody lighting, creative cocktails and shareable, beautifully plated Italian dishes. Book ahead, space is small and reservations are required for dinner.

**Mario's Italian Cafés** - A family business that has served the Coachella Valley for over 50 years, with locations in Palm Desert, La Quinta, Indio, Bermuda Dunes, and Sun City Palm Desert. Mario and his family have been delighting residents with old-world family recipes and authentic Italian food and pizza prepared at their award-winning restaurants.

**Ciro's Pizza (Indio)** - Voted Coachella Valley's favorite Italian restaurant and favorite pizza, Ciro's is the perfect place to enjoy a delicious meal with family and friends.

## Other Notable Options:

- **Your Pie** - They've got some of the best caprese pizza, sandwiches, and tomato mozzarella salads around
- **Civico Restaurants** - Offering modern Italian/Californian fare including vegan-friendly options

These restaurants offer a range of dining experiences from casual pizzerias to upscale speakeasy-style establishments.

Links: [{"title":"Where to Go for Date Night in the Coachella Valley | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/where-to-go-for-date-night-in-the-coachella-valley/"},{"title":"Diners' Choice: Romantic restaurants in Palm Springs / Palm Desert","url":"https://www.opentable.com/s/dinerschoice?metroid=81&regionids=82&topic=Romance"},{"title":"26 Best Date Night Restaurants in Coachella | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-restaurants-for-date-night-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-coachella"},{"title":"161 Best Date Night Restaurants in Palm Springs, Palm Springs / Palm Desert Cities | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-date-night-restaurants-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"14 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs to Book Right Now","url":"https://www.timeout.com/palm-springs/restaurants/best-restaurants-in-palm-springs"},{"title":"The 28 best romantic restaurants in Palm Springs","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/79311/best-romantic-restaurants-in-palm-springs"},{"title":"131 Best Date Night Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-date-night-restaurants-palm-desert-ca"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best date night restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Palm Springs area:

## Fine Dining & Upscale Options

**Copley's** is an award-winning restaurant serving American cuisine infused with hints of tropical and European flavors, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate in the heart of Palm Springs.

**Lavender Bistro** combines fine meats and seafood with fresh organic ingredients, offering indoor dining in a warm atmosphere or on a stunning tree-lined patio, with live music nightly.

**La Quinta Cliffhouse** juts from a massive rock outcropping and is renowned for spectacular valley views, offering American classics with innovative preparations focused on premium steaks and seafood.

**Melvyn's**, located at the legendary Ingleside Estate hotel, is elegantly upbeat and offers martinis, live music, weekend brunch, and sophisticated dining.

## International Cuisine

**FARM** is a Provencial-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with a menu of traditional European dishes featuring locally sourced produce and artisanal meats, served at a leisurely pace in an outdoor setting reminiscent of southern France.

**Mikado** at the JW Marriott Desert Springs Resort & Spa is known as the leader in authentic Teppan-Yaki Dining, with an ultimate sushi bar where you can enjoy the freshest foods.

## Unique & Contemporary Experiences

**Azúcar** offers breathtaking rooftop views with vibrant cuisine and craft cocktails, celebrating fresh California ingredients and Latin culinary traditions, located in historic La Serena Villas in Downtown Palm Springs.

**Tommy Bahama Miramonte** features an enchanting patio with twinkling lights and a romantic, California-meets-France menu.

During peak tourist season (winter and early spring), local eateries are filled to capacity, so it's always a good idea to make reservations.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Ciro's and Italian dining in the Indio/Palm Springs area:

## Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria in Indio

Ciro's Ristorante and Pizzeria was opened in 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella. The Mazzellas moved to the desert after many years as owners and operators of successful Italian Restaurants in New Jersey.

The restaurant has been voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant and Favorite Pizza. Ciro's pizza dough is prepared fresh daily from scratch and hand tossed.

## "That's Amore" Italian Article from Palm Springs Life

The search also returned a Palm Springs Life article titled "That's Amore!" about Italian restaurants in the greater Palm Springs area. This article discusses the history of Italian dining in Palm Springs, including references to establishments like Martoni's (featuring piano performances by Jack Lemmon), Johnny Costa's Ristorante, and various other Italian restaurants that have shaped the region's culinary scene. The article explores how traditional Italian restaurants have persevered in the area, emphasizing authentic ingredients and preparation methods.

content.text:
Note: TripAdvisor ranking appears as #22 in one fetch and #27 in another search result — they may differ by source/date. The claim of #22 of 165 matches the direct TripAdvisor fetch.

I have enough verification data now. Key findings:

- TripAdvisor: 79 reviews confirmed, #22 of 165 (direct fetch) — matches claim
- Yelp: 344 reviews confirmed
- Restaurantji: 161 reviews confirmed
- Founded 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella (from NJ), Ron and Norma Hare current operators — confirmed
- "Voted CV's Favorite Italian Restaurant & Favorite Pizza" — appears only in self-description echoed across listings; no independent award body found
- Coachella Valley Locals best Italian in Indio list: Ciro's is third, NOT first (claim says "named first") — contradicts
- Palm Springs Life "That's Amore" article: 403 blocked, cannot confirm absence directly
- Best pizza CV lists: Ciro's does not appear in TripAdvisor/Yelp/NBC top pizza lists — confirms absence
- Date night CV lists: Ciro's absent from Palm Springs Life, OpenTable, Timeout date night lists — confirms
- Own site cirosindio.com: socket closed on multiple attempts — confirms intermittent/blocked
- Google rating ~4.3 — one search snippet confirms this number

confirms: Yelp ~344 reviews (confirmed: Yelp page title shows 155 Photos & 344 Reviews, updated Feb 2026)
TripAdvisor ~79 reviews (confirmed via direct fetch: 79 reviews, 4.0 stars)
Restaurantji ~161 reviews (confirmed via search result snippet showing 'Reviews (161)')
Google rating ~4.3 (confirmed via search snippet: 'ranked 4.3 within the Google grading system')
Founded 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella, relocated from NJ (confirmed via multiple sources echoing the same origin story)
Current operators Ron and Norma Hare continue tradition (confirmed in search results)

TripAdvisor ranking #22 of 165 Indio restaurants (confirmed via direct TripAdvisor fetch; note one search snippet said #27 of 133, suggesting the count fluctuates, but #22 of 165 matches the live listing)
Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listing (confirmed: listing URL exists at visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/ciros-ristorante-&-pizzeria/6903/)

Surfaces for 'Italian restaurant Coachella Valley' / 'best Italian Indio' queries (confirmed: appears in Coachella Valley Locals list, named in Visit Greater Palm Springs results, surfaced in AI summary of best Italian)

Does NOT surface for 'best pizza Coachella Valley' top lists (confirmed: TripAdvisor top pizza, Yelp top pizza Palm Desert/Coachella, NBC Palm Springs 'best pies' list all omit Ciro's; Coachella Pizza Co, Sabatinos, TKB, Your Pie, Stuft, Popn Off, Billy Q's, Mario's surface instead)

Does NOT surface for 'date night Coachella Valley' (confirmed: Palm Springs Life date night list, OpenTable date night lists, Timeout, Wanderlog romantic lists feature Copley's, Lavender Bistro, Melvyn's, FARM, Tommy Bahama, Azucar, La Quinta Cliffhouse — no Ciro's)
Own domain cirosindio.com fails to load via WebFetch (confirmed: socket closed on both https and http attempts, consistent with intermittent availability or bot-blocking)

NAP scattered across many third-party aggregators with name variants (confirmed: Yelp shows 'CIROS RISTORANTE', TripAdvisor 'Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria', Restaurantji 'Ciros Restorante', OpenTable has two separate listings — ciros-restorante-indio AND ciros-indio (the latter categorized as 'American Restaurant') — indicating duplicate/inconsistent listings)
contradicts: Claim: 'named first on Coachella Valley Locals best-of list' for best Italian in Indio. Actually: Ciro's appears THIRD on the Coachella Valley Locals 'Best Italian Food in Indio' list, after Coachella Pizza Company (1) and Mario's Italian Cafes (2). It is followed by Your Pie (4) and TKB Bakery & Deli (5).

Claim: TripAdvisor rating implicit in 'mostly positive' framing. Actually: TripAdvisor average is 4.0/5, not as strong as the Yelp/Google read implies — consistent with the 'mixed-to-declining recent reviews' caveat but worth noting the TripAdvisor average is the weakest of the platforms cited.
flagged_as_unverified: 'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant' and 'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Pizza' — these appear only as self-description echoed on Visit Greater Palm Springs, TripAdvisor business description, and aggregator listings. No independent award sponsor, year, or publication source identified (e.g., not traced to Palm Springs Life Best Of, Desert Sun reader poll, or a named CV publication). Likely a long-standing self-claim that has propagated across listings.
Schema.org / JSON-LD presence on cirosindio.com — site would not load (socket closed on both https and http), cannot confirm or deny structured data.
Google review count remains unknown — only the 4.3 rating number surfaced; no count verified.

Sentiment 'mixed-to-declining in recent reviews' — could not pull recent review bodies directly (Yelp and individual TripAdvisor reviews returned 403); TripAdvisor's 4.0 average is consistent with mixed sentiment but not directly verified at the review level.

Absence from Palm Springs Life 'That's Amore' Italian roundup — article returned HTTP 403 so I could not directly confirm the restaurant list. The search result summary of that article mentioned Martoni's, Johnny Costa's, and others, with no mention of Ciro's, which is suggestive but not direct confirmation.
Stale 'CLOSED' flag on Yellow Pages — Yellow Pages listing exists but I did not directly inspect its open/closed status indicator.
input.notes: The visibility assessment is broadly accurate: review-volume numbers match, longevity story is well-corroborated, the 'surfaces for Italian / does not surface for pizza or date night' pattern is independently confirmed, and the broken own-site is real. The most material correction is the 'named first on Coachella Valley Locals' claim — Ciro's is actually third on that list (behind Coachella Pizza Company and Mario's), which slightly weakens the 'strong surfacing for best Italian in Indio' framing. The 'voted favorite' credentials should be treated as self-reported marketing copy, not independent awards, since no sponsoring publication or year is traceable. Visibility gap score of 7 and convertible tier remain defensible given the broken site, duplicate OpenTable listings (one miscategorized as 'American'), inconsistent name spellings across aggregators, and absence from pizza/date-night queries despite 60-year longevity.
